summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a high‑energy late‑80s show, highlighted by a scorching Scarlet > Fire and a seamless, flowing second set.
review: The recording is praised for its crystal‑clear soundboard quality that even rivals the FM broadcast, making every instrument shine. Fans describe the concert as electric – from the opening Hell in a Bucket to the unforgettable Scarlet > Fire, with standout moments like Terrapin Station and an inspired Watchtower reading, while the second set flows effortlessly. A few reviewers note the performance isn’t as flawless as other nights, but overall the show is celebrated for its energy and memorable jams.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviews (6/7) are 4‑5 stars and highlight both superb soundboard quality and strong musical moments; only one moderate review tempers the praise, resulting in a solid above‑average rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as crisp, great, and on par with or better than the FM broadcast; no dropout or muffling issues reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers note a high‑energy opening, seamless transitions in the second set, and an especially uplifting Fantasy > Hey Jude fade‑out; overall pacing is described as flowing effortlessly.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mentioned as "flowing seamlessly" with his guitar work throughout the second set.
band_member_comments.Bob: His unorthodox reading of Watchtower gave reviewers chills and was highlighted as a unique moment.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboardist) described as phenomenal, especially on vocal parts for Mr. Fantasy.
